skill.name: cdk-patterns
skill.description: Guides AWS CDK design using reusable stacks, L2 constructs, least-privilege IAM, monitoring, and production-ready infrastructure patterns.
skill.summary: Design reusable AWS CDK stacks and constructs with production-ready defaults.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported dynamic execution, shell backticks, and weak cryptography patterns. Manual review found these are false positives from AWS CDK code examples, Markdown formatting, and ordinary words such as description and designing. No prompt injection, exfiltration, or malicious execution behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False positive: AWS CDK Function construct
low_findings.description: The high-severity dynamic function finding at SKILL.md line 53 refers to lambda.Function in a CDK TypeScript example. It is an infrastructure construct declaration, not JavaScript dynamic code execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding lines define an AWS Lambda resource with runtime, handler, code asset, environment, and tracing fields. No evidence of the JavaScript Function constructor or user-controlled code execution was found.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown backticks
low_findings.description: The medium-severity external command findings at SKILL.md lines 29, 37, 66, 70, 72, and 73 are Markdown inline code or fenced code examples. They do not execute commands during skill use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected characters are Markdown delimiters around CDK terms and an example code block. No shell interpolation, subprocess call, or execution instruction was found.

low_findings.title: False positive: Weak cryptography keyword match
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography findings at SKILL.md lines 3 and 14 appear to match ordinary prose in the skill description and use-case text. The file contains no cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found AWS CDK documentation text at both locations, not MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or another weak cryptographic implementation. No evidence of cryptographic behavior was found.

content.value_statement: Teams often repeat fragile infrastructure code across AWS CDK projects. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design reusable CDK patterns with safer production defaults.
actual_capabilities: Identifies suitable CDK patterns for serverless APIs, container services, static sites, and data pipelines.
Recommends reusable stack and construct boundaries for multi-stack CDK applications.
Prioritizes L2 constructs over L1 constructs when safer abstractions exist.
Guides least-privilege IAM grants, tagging, retention policies, and monitoring defaults.
Reviews CDK designs for common anti-patterns such as hardcoded accounts and weak stack coupling.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not live AWS account inspection.
It does not replace cdk synth, cdk diff, security scans, or deployment tests.
Examples may need adaptation for each organization's compliance and networking standards.
It focuses on AWS CDK rather than Terraform, Pulumi, or raw CloudFormation.

use_cases.title: Create a serverless API construct
use_cases.description: Design a reusable API Gateway, Lambda, and DynamoDB pattern with retention, tracing, and least-privilege access.

use_cases.title: Review production CDK stacks
use_cases.description: Check stack boundaries, IAM grants, tagging, monitoring, and resource lifecycle settings before a release.

use_cases.title: Standardize infrastructure templates
use_cases.description: Convert repeated CDK snippets into shared L3 constructs that teams can reuse across services.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a CDK pattern
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a new AWS service
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose an AWS CDK pattern for a new service. The service needs [requirements], expected traffic is [traffic], and data storage is [storage].

prompt_templates.title: Design a reusable construct
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a reusable CDK construct for [infrastructure pattern]. Include stack boundaries, required props, IAM grants, monitoring, and production defaults.

prompt_templates.title: Review an existing stack
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this CDK stack for reusable design, least-privilege IAM, stateful resource handling, monitoring, and deployment risks: [paste stack summary].

prompt_templates.title: Refactor multi-stack architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this multi-stack CDK application into cleaner stateful, stateless, and shared construct layers. Explain dependency boundaries and migration steps.

output_examples.input: I need a reusable serverless API pattern for Lambda and DynamoDB.
output_examples.output: A suitable output would define the construct boundary, required properties, table retention strategy, Lambda tracing, API Gateway settings, and least-privilege table access.

output_examples.input: Review my CDK architecture before production deployment.
output: Stateful resources should be separated from stateless compute stacks.
IAM access should use construct grants instead of broad custom policies.
Monitoring, tagging, and cdk diff should be part of the release checklist.

output_examples.input: Our teams copy the same bucket and Lambda setup into every service.
output_examples.output: A suitable output would propose a shared L3 construct with configurable props, safe defaults, consistent tags, retention policy guidance, and test expectations.

best_practices: Provide the target AWS service pattern, language, environment, and production constraints before asking for designs.
Ask for stack boundaries, IAM scope, monitoring, tagging, and lifecycle policies in every CDK design review.
Validate generated guidance with cdk synth, cdk diff, tests, and your organization's security controls.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill for Terraform, Pulumi, or raw CloudFormation-specific implementation work.
Do not deploy generated infrastructure guidance without review, synthesis, and environment-specific validation.
Do not ask for one large stack when stateful and stateless resources should be separated.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps design reusable AWS CDK stacks, L2 and L3 constructs, and common cloud infrastructure patterns.

faq.question: Which CDK languages does it mention?
faq.answer: The skill description references TypeScript, Python, and Java, with examples shown in TypeScript.

faq.question: Can it deploy resources to AWS?
faq.answer: No. It provides design guidance and examples. Deployment still requires your CDK project, AWS credentials, and normal release process.

faq.question: Does it cover least-privilege IAM?
faq.answer: Yes. The instructions explicitly prioritize least-privilege IAM roles, policies, and construct grant methods.

faq.question: Is it suitable for production CDK reviews?
faq.answer: Yes, as a guidance aid. You should still run cdk synth, cdk diff, tests, and security reviews.

faq.question: Does it support non-CDK infrastructure tools?
faq.answer: No. It is focused on AWS CDK and should not be used for Terraform-specific or raw CloudFormation tasks.
